title: galium palustre f. rupicola
intro: Galium palustre f. rupicola, also known as alpine bedstraw, is a subspecies of the Galium palustre plant, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the bedstraw family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, digestive problems, and urinary tract infections. The health benefits of Galium palustre f. rupicola include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and help in the management of kidney stones, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Galium palustre f. rupicola can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes.
